basic_details.job_title: IIT Ropar Project Research Scientist Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Institute of Technology Ropar (IIT Ropar)
basic_details.short_information: Indian Institute of Technology Ropar (IIT Ropar) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 1 Project Research Scientist I (Non Medical) Posts. Interested and eligible candidates can apply Online through the official IIT Ropar website. The last date to submit the application form is 24-06-2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 35 years Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

qualification.education_qualification: B.E./B.Tech in CS/AI/Data Science/IT (first class + GATE unless CFTI), M.Tech/M.E. in CS/Applied Mathematics/AI/Data Science/IT (first class), M.Sc. in Data Science/Data Analytics (with valid GATE/UGC-NET/CSIR/DST-INSPIRE)
qualification.details: Project Research Scientist I (Non Medical): B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science/Artificial Intelligence/Data Science/Information Technology (first class, min 75% or 7.5 CGPA) with valid GATE/UGC-NET/CSIR/DST-INSPIRE unless from CFTI; or M.Tech/M.E. in relevant domains (first class, min 60% or 6.0 CGPA); or M.Sc. in Data Science/Data Analytics with valid GATE/UGC-NET/CSIR/DST-INSPIRE. Must enroll as self-sponsored PhD student at IIT Ropar.

how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the IIT Ropar Project Research Scientist I (Non Medical) Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 24-06-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of IIT Ropar to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the IIT Ropar Project Research Scientist I (Non Medical) recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application form is 24th June 2026. Ensure your application is submitted before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy for the post of Project Research Scientist I (Non Medical) in this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates need a B.E./B.Tech, M.Tech/M.E., or M.Sc. degree in relevant fields with specific first-class marks and valid GATE/UGC-NET/CSIR/DST-INSPIRE scores, or be from a CFTI.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the applicants?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for applicants is 35 years. Age relaxation will be provided as per government norms.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Project Research Scientist I (Non Medical) position?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 56,000 plus HRA per month.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) in the official notification.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process will primarily involve an Online Interview. Shortlisting will be done based on the applications received.

faq.question: Q8. What is the mode of application for this job?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the official IIT Ropar website.

faq.question: Q9. Is this a permanent or contractual job?
faq.answer: This is a contractual job opportunity at IIT Ropar.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and apply online?
faq.answer: You can find the links to the official notification and the online application form in the "Important Links" section of this article.
